headline: Carnival and Norwegian Cruise Line Fall 4%: Fuel Costs Are Winning the Battle Against Booking Strength
summary: Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) stock retreated 4% in Thursday’s session, trading below $26 after opening at $26.58. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) stock also sank 4%, changing hands at less than $19. Both moves come on the last trading session before Good Friday, when the markets close for the holiday. The selling pressure reflects a sector-wide ... Carnival and Norwegian Cruise Line Fall 4%: Fuel Costs Are Winning the Battle Against Booking Strength

headline: Holland America Line Marks 153 Years of Exploration with Special 'Anniversary Sale'
summary: For 153 years, Holland America Line has perfected the art of leisurely travel, inviting guests to connect to the world and each other. This April, the brand celebrates with a monthlong Anniversary Sale featuring fare discounts, onboard credits, reduced deposits, and free fares for kids. Running from April 2–30, 2026, the Anniversary Sale includes departures from April 2026 through spring 2027, with cruises ranging from four to 69 days.

headline: Shares of cruise companies are trading lower as oil prices surge in response to President Trump's speech, where he indicated that the U.S. would continue strikes against Iran. However, he did not provide a timeline for conflict resolution.

headline: Oil Tops $110 As Trump Vows 'Stone Age' For Iran: 5 Stocks Hit Hardest On Thursday
summary: Trump's Iran speech spooked markets Thursday. Oil surged past $110 a barrel as analysts warned the Strait of Hormuz stays closed — and travel stocks paid the price.
